channel.title: World News, Today World News, Latest International News, World Breaking News, Trending News of World - Times of India
channel.description: World News Today: Read the latest world news headlines, Current International breaking news world wide. In depth analysis and top news headlines world wide.
channel.copyright: Copyright:(C) Tue, 09 Jun 2026 10:16:11 IST Bennett Coleman & Co. Ltd, http://in.indiatimes.com/policyterms/1554651.cms

image.title: World News, Today World News, Latest International News, World Breaking News, Trending News of World - Times of India

item.title: US army Apache gunship goes down near Strait of Hormuz: Report
item.description: A US military Apache helicopter went down near the Strait of Hormuz on Tuesday amidst escalating tensions with Iran. The incident occurred following a significant military campaign launched by Donald Trump's administration against the Iranian regime. The exact cause of the crash, whether enemy action or mechanical failure, remains unconfirmed.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Who is Oblivion? Viral Japan livestream sparks Johnny Somali comparisons after dramatic FamilyMart confrontation
item.description: Kick streamer Oblivion sparked controversy during a June 8 livestream in Japan after being removed from a FamilyMart store and questioned by police over filming. The incident happened while he was traveling with Ryan Garcia. Social media users compared Oblivion to Johnny Somali, while the episode also renewed conversations about privacy rules and streamer behavior in Japan.
item.dc:creator: Sumit Roy

item.title: Who is Joe Weller? British YouTuber opens up about finding God after years of inner battles
item.description: Joe Weller announced on June 8, 2026, that he has found God and feels at peace after years of mental health struggles. In an emotional social media video, the British YouTuber said faith in Jesus Christ changed his life after other pursuits failed to bring happiness. The announcement went viral, drawing both support and skepticism online.
item.dc:creator: Sumit Roy

item.title: Twitch streamer Kathelina’s controversy deepens as former best friend Snowcone speaks out after Kenneth’s cheating allegations
item.description: Twitch streamer Kathelina remains at the center of a major online controversy after allegations made by her former boyfriend, Kenneth. The story took another turn when former friend Snowcone shared personal claims about the situation. Her comments sparked criticism from many viewers, who accused her of seeking attention. Kathelina has not publicly responded and remains offline.
item.dc:creator: Sumit Roy

item.title: What is happening inside Chud the Builder’s jail cell? Livestreamer makes new claims from custody
item.description: Chud the Builder, whose real name is Dalton Eatherly, claims Montgomery County Jail staff took away his Bible and refused to return it after his release from solitary confinement. The allegation was shared online by Tayler Hansen and reposted on Eatherly’s X account. Meanwhile, Eatherly remains jailed on a $1 million bond while facing an attempted murder charge.
item.dc:creator: Sumit Roy

item.title: Kai Cenat begins search for students as Streamer University 2026 opens applications
item.description: Kai Cenat has officially opened applications for Streamer University 2026, his popular creator event. This weekend-long program, first launched in 2025, brings together aspiring and established content creators on a college campus to collaborate and learn. Following its immense success and over a million applications previously, the event aims to foster new talent and provide industry insights.
item.dc:creator: Sumit Roy

item.title: Who is Hector Delion, Penn Station stabbing suspect arrested after 5 people were injured?
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Ex-CIA officer accused of siphoning $40 million in gold bars claimed it was for...
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Kick streamer Oblivion’s Family Mart encounter sparks debate over livestream etiquette in Japan
item.description: Kick streamer Oblivion faced criticism after a livestream in Japan led to an uncomfortable confrontation in a Family Mart. A store worker escorted him out and called the police due to filming without permission, sparking comparisons to Johnny Somali. The incident highlights the importance of respecting local customs and privacy when livestreaming abroad.
item.dc:creator: Sumit Roy

item.title: “Why are you talking …?”: CDawgVA reveals unexpected MrBeast call story as Chris Broad’s old Logan Paul criticism returns
item.description: Connor “CDawgVA” Colquhoun claimed during a livestream that MrBeast dislikes being discussed by other major YouTubers and sometimes contacts them about it. He shared an alleged personal experience involving a planned call with the creator. The conversation also revived attention around Chris Broad’s earlier criticism of MrBeast’s partnership with Logan Paul. MrBeast has not responded publicly.
item.dc:creator: Sumit Roy

item.title: Luigi Mangione's sister lands top doctor role at US university ahead of his murder trial
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Who is Neeraj Sharma, Indian-American man set to lose his US citizenship for visa fraud? Never had any real job to offer
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: H-1B $100,000 fee struck down as illegal: What happens next?
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: US judge strikes down Donald Trump's $100k visa fee, calls it unlawful
item.description: A US federal judge has ruled against a $100,000 fee imposed by former President Donald Trump on new H-1B visas. The judge found the fee unlawful. This fee significantly increased the cost for highly skilled foreign workers seeking to enter the United States. The ruling came after a lawsuit filed by state attorneys general.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Indian-origin restaurant owner in Texas says she doesn’t hire Indians: 'Our team doesn’t...'
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: 'No visa laws that target India': What US official recently said on H-1B woes
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: US warplane strikes, disables oil tanker near Oman; crew of 24 Indians evacuated
item.description: An unladen oil tanker, MT Marivex, carrying 24 Indian crew members, was disabled and caught fire in the Gulf of Oman following a US military action. The US stated an F/A-18 Super Hornet fired a precision munition after the vessel allegedly violated a blockade of Iran's ports and ignored directions. All Indian seafarers were safely evacuated with Omani assistance.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Trump’s Middle East peace remains under construction and Israel and Iran shoot at each other
